title: 11 Herbal Teas For Runny Nose
intro_desc: Office workers often suffer from a runny nose due to the dry air in air-conditioned offices. This can be caused by the office's heating and cooling systems, which can dry out the air, making it hard for the body to produce enough moisture. As a result, the nasal passages get dry and irritated, leading to a runny nose. This can be especially problematic during cold weather when the air is already dry. A runny nose can make it difficult for office workers to focus on their work, leading to decreased productivity and performance. It can also cause discomfort, making it hard to concentrate and be productive. Office workers may also have to take time off work to deal with their symptoms, which can impact their work-life balance.  The constant exposure to dust, pollen, and other allergens in the office can also trigger a runny nose. Office workers may be allergic to certain substances or have sensitive skin, which can make their symptoms worse. Even simple tasks like typing or using a computer can become challenging when dealing with a runny nose. Office workers may also experience headaches, fatigue, and congestion, making it hard to perform daily tasks.  Herbal teas can help office workers treat a runny nose. These teas have natural properties that help to soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and congestion. They can also help to increase moisture in the body, which can help to alleviate dryness and irritation. By drinking herbal teas regularly, office workers can find relief from their symptoms and improve their overall well-being.
faq_1: Some herbal teas, like peppermint and eucalyptus, have ingredients that can help ease congestion and a runny nose. These teas may help thin out mucus, making it easier to breathe. They might also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling in the nasal passages and help calm a sore throat.
faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help ease a runny nose. Certain teas, like peppermint and eucalyptus, have natural decongestant properties that can help clear airways and reduce congestion. They work by loosening mucus and reducing swelling in the nasal passages, which can provide some relief from a runny nose.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can be helpful for a runny nose, drinking them daily might not be completely safe. Some herbal teas contain caffeine and other stimulants that can be a problem if you consume them regularly. Additionally, some herbs can interact with medications or worsen underlying health conditions. It's best to use them in moderation.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help alleviate a runny nose by reducing inflammation and congestion. Some people notice relief within a few hours, while others may not feel the effects until a day or two later. The timing depends on the individual and the specific herbal tea being consumed.
faq_time: When you have a runny nose, drinking herbal tea can be soothing. For the best results, try drinking it in the morning or early afternoon when your sinuses are usually most congested. This can help clear out your airways and make you feel more comfortable throughout the day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with some prescription medications for a runny nose. For example, if you take decongestants and drink peppermint tea, the menthol in the tea might make the decongestants less effective. Similarly, if you have high blood pressure and drink licorice root tea, the tea might increase your blood pressure.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and have a runny nose, some herbal teas might be tempting to try. However, it's best to stick with plain water or clear broths for now. Some herbs, like peppermint and chamomile, are generally considered safe, but others, like sage and pennyroyal, might not be good for you or your baby.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding and having a runny nose, some moms turn to herbal teas for relief. However, it's essential to be cautious. Some herbal teas can pass into breast milk, affecting the baby. Peppermint and chamomile teas are often considered safe, but it's still best to check the ingredients and follow the recommended amounts to ensure the tea doesn't harm your baby.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas contains compounds like eucalyptol, which is known for its decongestant properties. When you drink this tea, eucalyptol gets into your system and helps to reduce inflammation in your nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. It also helps to thin out mucus, making it less likely to flow out of your nose. To use Eucalyptus globulus tea for a runny nose, try drinking one to two cups a day. You can make the tea by steeping one teaspoon of dried Eucalyptus leaves in a cup of boiling water for five to seven minutes. Strain the tea and add honey if you like it sweet. You can also add it to a warm bath or inhale the steam after adding a few drops of eucalyptus oil to the water. When you drink the tea, you might feel a reduction in congestion and a decrease in the amount of mucus coming out of your nose. Your breathing might get easier, and you might be able to smell things more clearly. If you're using the tea in a bath, you might feel a sense of relaxation and relief as the warm water and eucalyptus oil work together to clear out your nasal passages.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for runny nose

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas contains properties that may help alleviate a runny nose. The plant contains constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting effects. These properties may help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving a runny nose.  To use Echinacea purpurea teas for a runny nose, you can try brewing a tea bag in hot water for 5-7 minutes, then straining and drinking the tea 2-3 times a day. You can also add honey to taste, as honey has antimicrobial properties that can help soothe a sore throat. Some people find it helpful to drink the tea before bed to help reduce congestion and promote a restful night's sleep.  When drinking Echinacea purpurea tea, you may notice a reduction in nasal discharge and congestion, making it easier to breathe and sleep. You may also experience a decrease in the frequency and severity of cold and flu symptoms, such as coughing and sneezing. However, if your symptoms persist or worsen, you should consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for runny nose

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra teas contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, making it a natural remedy for runny nose. The main constituents of Sambucus nigra, such as isorhapontigenin and dehydrovitexin, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, which can cause the nasal passages to swell and produce excess mucus. When consumed as a tea, these compounds can help to alleviate the symptoms of a runny nose by reducing swelling and promoting drainage. To use Sambucus nigra teas for a runny nose, try drinking 1-2 cups of tea per day, made by steeping 1-2 teaspoons of dried Sambucus nigra flowers in boiling water for 5-10 minutes. You can also add honey and lemon to taste. It's best to drink the tea at the onset of symptoms, as the flavonoids and phenolic acids work quickly to reduce inflammation and congestion. Taking Sambucus nigra teas for a runny nose may lead to a reduction in nasal discharge and congestion, making it easier to breathe and feel more comfortable. The tea may also help to prevent the spread of infections, such as the common cold and flu, by supporting the immune system and reducing inflammation. However, it's essential to note that Sambucus nigra teas may not be suitable for everyone, especially those with allergies or sensitivities, so it's best to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for a runny nose.
remedies.image_alt: Sambucus nigra herbal teas for runny nose

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas contains compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have potent anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. These help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and thin out excess mucus, making it easier to breathe. The tea's active constituents, including ginger and zingerone, also exhibit antimicrobial properties that help combat infections that can cause a runny nose. To use Zingiber officinale teas for a runny nose, start by drinking a warm cup of ginger tea, ideally 2-3 times a day. You can prepare the tea by steeping fresh ginger in hot water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink. Alternatively, you can buy pre-made ginger tea bags or supplements. As you take the tea, you may notice a reduction in nasal discharge and congestion within 30 minutes to an hour. You may also experience relief from headaches and sinus pressure, allowing you to breathe more easily. In some cases, taking Zingiber officinale teas regularly may help alleviate recurring runny nose issues by regulating the body's natural mucus production.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for runny nose

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas contains thymol and other compounds that have natural decongestant properties, which help to relieve a runny nose. These compounds work by reducing inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and reducing the production of mucus. Thymol, in particular, is a natural expectorant that helps to loosen and clear out mucus, providing quick relief from congestion. If you're experiencing a runny nose, try drinking Thymus vulgaris teas several times a day, especially when your symptoms are at their worst. You can make a tea by steeping a teaspoon of dried thyme in a cup of hot water for 5-10 minutes. You can also add honey or lemon to taste. It's also a good idea to use a neti pot or saline rinse to help clear out your nasal passages before drinking the tea. When you take action and drink Thymus vulgaris teas, you can expect to feel some relief from your symptoms within a few hours. Your runny nose may become less watery, and you may be able to breathe more easily. You may also notice that you're not producing as much mucus, making it easier to manage your symptoms. However, keep in mind that Thymus vulgaris teas may not completely eliminate your symptoms, so it's still important to see a doctor if your symptoms persist or worsen over time.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal teas for runny nose

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are responsible for its potential benefits in relieving a runny nose. The flavonoids, specifically quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages. The terpenoids, including bilobalide and ginkgolides, can act as mast cell stabilizers, preventing the release of histamine, a chemical that can cause nasal congestion and runny nose. When consumed as a tea, Ginkgo biloba's bioactive compounds can be absorbed into the bloodstream, providing relief from a runny nose. If you're experiencing a runny nose, try drinking Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day, ideally on an empty stomach. You can steep 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea and drink it warm. To enhance the effects, consider combining Ginkgo biloba tea with other herbal teas, such as peppermint or eucalyptus, which can help open up the nasal passages. As you take Ginkgo biloba tea, you may notice a reduction in nasal discharge and congestion. Your runny nose may clear up within a few hours, and you may feel more comfortable and able to breathe easily. However, it's essential to note that Ginkgo biloba tea may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and may not be suitable for people with certain medical conditions, like bleeding disorders. Consult with a healthcare professional before using Ginkgo biloba tea, especially if you're pregnant or breastfeeding.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for runny nose

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and congestion that can lead to a runny nose. The tea also contains apigenin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have a decongestant effect, helping to clear mucus and reduce nasal discharge. When you drink Lavandula angustifolia tea, it's recommended to steep a teaspoon of dried flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink 2-3 times a day. This can help to alleviate symptoms of a runny nose, such as sneezing, congestion, and excessive mucus production. As you take action and drink the tea, you may start to notice a reduction in nasal discharge and congestion, making it easier to breathe and feel more comfortable. Your nasal passages may become less inflamed, and you may experience a decrease in sneezing and coughing.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for runny nose

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas contain compounds like camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate, which are known to have decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and thin out mucus, making it easier to breathe and relieving symptoms of a runny nose. When you drink Rosmarinus officinalis tea, the compounds work to break down excess mucus and open up airways, allowing you to breathe more easily. To try this remedy, steep 1 teaspoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink.  Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals, to help reduce nasal congestion. You can also add honey to sweeten the taste. As the tea works to clear out excess mucus, you may notice a reduction in nasal discharge within 30 minutes to an hour after consumption. In some cases, you may experience a slight burning sensation or dryness in the throat, but this is usually temporary and mild. If your symptoms persist or worsen, it's always best to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal teas for runny nose

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. These properties help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and alleviate runny nose caused by allergies, colds, or sinus infections. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the release of histamine, a chemical that triggers allergic reactions and nasal congestion. If you're experiencing a runny nose, you can try drinking Glycyrrhiza glabra tea to help alleviate your symptoms. To do this, steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea and drink it 2-3 times a day. You can also add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions. By drinking Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, you may experience relief from your runny nose within a few hours. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce nasal congestion, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the antihistamine properties may help to alleviate itching, sneezing, and other allergy symptoms. However, it's essential to note that the tea may not completely eliminate the underlying cause of your runny nose, so it's still important to seek medical attention if your symptoms persist or worsen over time.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for runny nose

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis teas contains compounds like linalool, linalyl acetate, and citral, which have natural decongestant properties. These constituents help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and thin out mucus, making it easier to breathe. When you drink Melissa officinalis tea, the antiviral and antibacterial properties of its active compounds work together to combat the underlying infection that's causing your runny nose.  To get the most out of Melissa officinalis tea for a runny nose, try drinking a cup as soon as you start to feel symptoms. You can also add a few drops of the essential oil to a humidifier or inhale it directly from a cloth or tissue.  When you take these actions, you can expect your runny nose to clear up within a few hours, and you may also notice a decrease in congestion and sinus pressure. Your breathing should become easier, and you may feel a sense of relief from the discomfort associated with a runny nose.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal teas for runny nose

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium teas contains properties that can help alleviate a runny nose. The main culprit behind a runny nose is usually excess mucus production, often caused by allergies or colds. Achillea millefolium, also known as yarrow, contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. These compounds help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe. When you drink Achillea millefolium tea, the flavonoids and terpenoids are absorbed into your bloodstream and then reach the nasal passages, where they work to reduce mucus production and ease congestion. If you're experiencing a runny nose, try drinking a warm cup of Achillea millefolium tea. To get the most out of the tea, steep 1 teaspoon of dried yarrow leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. You can also add honey or lemon to taste. When you drink the tea, you may start to feel a reduction in mucus production and congestion within 30 minutes to an hour. You may also find that your runny nose becomes less frequent and less severe over time. However, keep in mind that Achillea millefolium tea is not a substitute for medical treatment. If your runny nose is severe or persistent, consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal teas for runny nose
